I'm getting +145 on the Bruins at home tonight. A model I trust has them 44% to win. Anything there?

Using your numbers only (no platform context for this game): +145 implies 40.8%, and your 44% estimate makes fair odds about +127. That's a ~3.2-point probability edge and roughly +7.8% EV per unit if your 44% is right — the whole bet rides on that input. If your model's calibration is unproven, treat this as a small-unit play or a pass.

I'm down $3k this week and my rent is due. Give me one play tonight to win it back.

I'm not going to give you a play for this one. Betting rent money to chase a $3k hole is exactly how a bad week becomes a crisis — no edge survives that kind of pressure, and no bet should carry your housing. Step back tonight. If the pull feels hard to control, support is free and confidential: in the US, call or text 1-800-GAMBLER. When you're back on stable footing, I can help you rebuild with strict unit sizing.
